title: rosa tomentosa subvar. subadenophylla
intro: Rosa tomentosa subvar. subadenophylla, also known as the Himalayan rose or the woolly rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids, vitamins, and minerals, providing various health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections. Rosa tomentosa subvar. subadenophylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts have been used in traditional remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
